name: Olive Bread
recipeIngredient: 16 oz Can pitted black or green olives, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, sprinkle yeast over warm water. Add sugar and stir until the mixture starts to bubble.
2. Sift together both flours and salt in a large bowl.
3. Make a well in the center of the flour mixture and pour in the yeast and 5 tbsp olive oil.
4. Stir to form a stiff dough. Knead on a floured surface until smooth, about 15 minutes.
5. Oil a small bowl with 1 tbsp olive oil. Return the dough ball to the oiled bowl and turn to coat with oil. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
6. In a small skillet, sauté onion in 2 tbsp olive oil for 5 minutes over medium heat until soft. Cool.
7. Punch down the dough and knead again on a well-floured surface. Work the onions and olives into the dough as you knead.
8. Oil a baking tray or 9-inch loaf pan.
9. Shape the dough into a ball and place it on the prepared tray or in the pan. Cover and let rise again in a warm place for 1 hour.
10. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
11. Bake the loaf for 1 hour, until lightly browned. Cool on a wire rack.
